english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus, Apostolus Jesu Christi per voluntatem Dei, et Timotheus frater, ecclesiæ Dei, quæ est Corinthi cum omnibus sanctis, qui sunt in universa Achaia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος ἀπόστολος ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ καὶ τιμόθεος ὁ ἀδελφός τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ τῇ οὔσῃ ἐν κορίνθῳ σὺν τοῖς ἁγίοις πᾶσιν τοῖς οὖσιν ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ ἀχαΐᾳ
verses.king_james: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy *our* brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia vobis, et pax a Deo Patre nostro, et Domino Jesu Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν καὶ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Grace *be* to you and peace from God our Father, and *from* the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Benedictus Deus et Pater Domini nostri Jesu Christi, Pater misericordiarum, et Deus totius consolationis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐλογητὸς ὁ θεὸς καὶ πατὴρ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ὁ πατὴρ τῶν οἰκτιρμῶν καὶ θεὸς πάσης παρακλήσεως
verses.king_james: Blessed *be* God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui consolatur nos in omni tribulatione nostra: ut possimus et ipsi consolari eos qui in omni pressura sunt, per exhortationem, qua exhortamur et ipsi a Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ παρακαλῶν ἡμᾶς ἐπὶ πάσῃ τῇ θλίψει ἡμῶν εἰς τὸ δύνασθαι ἡμᾶς παρακαλεῖν τοὺς ἐν πάσῃ θλίψει διὰ τῆς παρακλήσεως ἡς παρακαλούμεθα αὐτοὶ ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam sicut abundant passiones Christi in nobis: ita et per Christum abundat consolatio nostra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι καθὼς περισσεύει τὰ παθήματα τοῦ χριστοῦ εἰς ἡμᾶς οὕτως διὰ χριστοῦ περισσεύει καὶ ἡ παράκλησις ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sive autem tribulamur pro vestra exhortatione et salute, sive consolamur pro vestra consolatione, sive exhortamur pro vestra exhortatione et salute, quæ operatur tolerantiam earumdem passionum, quas et nos patimur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε δὲ θλιβόμεθα ὑπὲρ τῆς ὑμῶν παρακλήσεως καὶ σωτηρίας τῆς ἐνεργουμένης ἐν ὑπομονῇ τῶν αὐτῶν παθημάτων ὧν καὶ ἡμεῖς πάσχομεν εἴτε παρακαλούμεθα ὑπὲρ τῆς ὑμῶν παρακλήσεως καὶ σωτηρίας, καὶ ἥ ἐλπίς ἡμῶν βεβαία ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And whether we be afflicted, *it is* for your consolation and salvation, which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, *it is* for your consolation and salvation.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut spes nostra firma sit pro vobis: scientes quod sicut socii passionum estis, sic eritis et consolationis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδότες ὅτι ὥσπερ κοινωνοί ἐστε τῶν παθημάτων οὕτως καὶ τῆς παρακλήσεως
verses.king_james: And our hope of you *is* stedfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so *shall ye be* also of the consolation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim volumus ignorare vos, fratres, de tribulatione nostra, quæ facta est in Asia, quoniam supra modum gravati sumus supra virtutem, ita ut tæderet nos etiam vivere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ θέλομεν ὑμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν ἀδελφοί ὑπὲρ τῆς θλίψεως ἡμῶν τῆς γενομένης ἡμῖν ἐν τῇ ἀσίᾳ ὅτι καθ᾽ ὑπερβολὴν ἐβαρήθημεν ὑπὲρ δύναμιν ὥστε ἐξαπορηθῆναι ἡμᾶς καὶ τοῦ ζῆν
verses.king_james: For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we despaired even of life:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ipsi in nobismetipsis responsum mortis habuimus, ut non simus fidentes in mortuos, sed in Deo, qui suscitat mortuos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ αὐτοὶ ἐν ἑαυτοῖς τὸ ἀπόκριμα τοῦ θανάτου ἐσχήκαμεν ἵνα μὴ πεποιθότες ὦμεν ἐφ᾽ ἑαυτοῖς ἀλλ᾽ ἐπὶ τῷ θεῷ τῷ ἐγείροντι τοὺς νεκρούς
verses.king_james: But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which raiseth the dead:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui de tantis periculis nos eripuit, et eruit: in quem speramus quoniam et adhuc eripiet,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἐκ τηλικούτου θανάτου ἐῤῥύσατο ἡμᾶς καὶ ῥύεται, εἰς ὃν ἠλπίκαμεν ὅτι καὶ ἔτι ῥύσεται
verses.king_james: Who delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver *us*;

verses.latin_vulgate: adjuvantibus et vobis in oratione pro nobis: ut ex multorum personis, ejus quæ in nobis est donationis, per multos gratiæ agantur pro nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνυπουργούντων καὶ ὑμῶν ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν τῇ δεήσει ἵνα ἐκ πολλῶν προσώπων τὸ εἰς ἡμᾶς χάρισμα διὰ πολλῶν εὐχαριστηθῇ ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Ye also helping together by prayer for us, that for the gift *bestowed* upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam gloria nostra hæc est: testimonium conscientiæ nostræ, quod in simplicitate cordis et sinceritate Dei, et non in sapientia carnali, sed in gratia Dei, conversati sumus in hoc mundo: abundantius autem ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ καύχησις ἡμῶν αὕτη ἐστίν τὸ μαρτύριον τῆς συνειδήσεως ἡμῶν ὅτι ἐν ἁπλότητι καὶ εἰλικρινείᾳ θεοῦ οὐκ ἐν σοφίᾳ σαρκικῇ ἀλλ᾽ ἐν χάριτι θεοῦ ἀνεστράφημεν ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ περισσοτέρως δὲ πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: For our rejoicing is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim alia scribimus vobis, quam quæ legistis, et cognovistis. Spero autem quod usque in finem cognoscetis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἄλλα γράφομεν ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ ἢ ἃ ἀναγινώσκετε ἢ καὶ ἐπιγινώσκετε ἐλπίζω δὲ ὅτι καὶ ἕως τέλους ἐπιγνώσεσθε
verses.king_james: For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut et cognovistis nos ex parte, quod gloria vestra sumus, sicut et vos nostra, in die Domini nostri Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς καὶ ἐπέγνωτε ἡμᾶς ἀπὸ μέρους ὅτι καύχημα ὑμῶν ἐσμεν καθάπερ καὶ ὑμεῖς ἡμῶν ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also *are* ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hac confidentia volui prius venire ad vos, ut secundum gratiam haberetis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταύτῃ τῇ πεποιθήσει ἐβουλόμην πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐλθεῖν πρότερον ἵνα δευτέραν χάριν ἔχῆτε
verses.king_james: And in this confidence I was minded to come unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;

verses.latin_vulgate: et per vos transire in Macedoniam, et iterum a Macedonia venire ad vos, et a vobis deduci in Judæam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ δι᾽ ὑμῶν διελθεῖν εἰς Μακεδονίαν καὶ πάλιν ἀπὸ Μακεδονίας ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς καὶ ὑφ᾽ ὑμῶν προπεμφθῆναι εἰς τὴν ἰουδαίαν
verses.king_james: And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judaea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo hoc voluissem, numquid levitate usus sum? aut quæ cogito, secundum carnem cogito, ut sit apud me Est et Non?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο οὖν βουλευόμενος μή τι ἄρα τῇ ἐλαφρίᾳ ἐχρησάμην ἢ ἃ βουλεύομαι κατὰ σάρκα βουλεύομαι ἵνα ᾖ παρ᾽ ἐμοὶ τὸ ναὶ ναὶ καὶ τὸ οὒ οὔ
verses.king_james: When I therefore was thus minded, did I use lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?

verses.latin_vulgate: Fidelis autem Deus, quia sermo noster, qui fuit apud vos, non est in illo Est et Non.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸς δὲ ὁ θεὸς ὅτι ὁ λόγος ἡμῶν ὁ πρὸς ὑμᾶς οὐκ ἐγένετο ναὶ καὶ οὔ
verses.king_james: But *as* God *is* true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dei enim Filius Jesus Christus, qui in vobis per nos prædicatus est, per me, et Silvanum, et Timotheum, non fuit Est et Non, sed Est in illo fuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ τοῦ θεοῦ υἱὸς ἰησοῦς χριστὸς ὁ ἐν ὑμῖν δι᾽ ἡμῶν κηρυχθείς δι᾽ ἐμοῦ καὶ σιλουανοῦ καὶ τιμοθέου οὐκ ἐγένετο ναὶ καὶ οὔ ἀλλὰ ναὶ ἐν αὐτῷ γέγονεν
verses.king_james: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, *even* by me and Silvanus and Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quotquot enim promissiones Dei sunt, in illo Est: ideo et per ipsum Amen Deo ad gloriam nostram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅσαι γὰρ ἐπαγγελίαι θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ τὸ ναί καὶ ἐν αὐτῷ τὸ ἀμὴν τῷ θεῷ πρὸς δόξαν δι᾽ ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: For all the promises of God in him *are* yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem confirmat nos vobiscum in Christo, et qui unxit nos Deus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ βεβαιῶν ἡμᾶς σὺν ὑμῖν εἰς χριστὸν καὶ χρίσας ἡμᾶς θεός
verses.king_james: Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath anointed us, *is* God;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui et signavit nos, et dedit pignus Spiritus in cordibus nostris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ καὶ σφραγισάμενος ἡμᾶς καὶ δοὺς τὸν ἀῤῥαβῶνα τοῦ πνεύματος ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Who hath also sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem testem Deum invoco in animam meam, quod parcens vobis, non veni ultra Corinthum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ μάρτυρα τὸν θεὸν ἐπικαλοῦμαι ἐπὶ τὴν ἐμὴν ψυχήν ὅτι φειδόμενος ὑμῶν οὐκέτι ἦλθον εἰς κόρινθον
verses.king_james: Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.

verses.latin_vulgate: non quia dominamur fidei vestæ, sed adjutores sumus gaudii vestri: nam fide statis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ ὅτι κυριεύομεν ὑμῶν τῆς πίστεως ἀλλὰ συνεργοί ἐσμεν τῆς χαρᾶς ὑμῶν τῇ γὰρ πίστει ἑστήκατε
verses.king_james: Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by faith ye stand.
